steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks which choice most logically completes the text. The experiment shows that caterpillars previously primed with a simulated attack showed defensive behaviors (coiling or flailing) when exposed to slight ambient electric field shifts, while a control group resumed neutral behavior almost immediately. The completion must explain why the treatment group reacted defensively to those field shifts.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'some species of caterpillars are able to detect potential threats via changes in ambient electric fields.': This directly explains the result — the treatment group detected the electric field shifts as a potential threat and responded defensively. ✓
  • Choice B — 'in some caterpillar species, the type and duration of defensive responses to electric field shifts varies based on the size of the shifts.': The experiment does not vary the size of the shifts, so this comparison is unsupported by the data described. ✗
  • Choice C — 'European peacock butterfly caterpillars are more sensitive to changes in ambient electric fields than are cinnabar moth caterpillars or scarce vapourer caterpillars.': No species-to-species sensitivity comparison is described in the experiment. ✗
  • Choice D — 'caterpillar species with the sensory acuity to detect small changes in ambient electric fields may also be able to cause shifts in those fields.': The ability to cause field shifts is never tested or implied. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
